commit.message:
SignaturesImpl: tolerate empty identifier after '.' in class type signatures

Follow-up to JDK-8341608. The Scala compiler emits class type signatures
with an empty identifier after the inner class separator '.', such as
`LOuter<TK;TV;>.;`. While this violates JVMS 4.7.9.1, such signatures
exist widely in published Scala artifacts and cannot be recompiled.

Make the identifier after '.' optional when an outer type is already
present, so that tools like jdeps no longer crash on these signatures.
